steps.title: Identify the underlined claim's causal logic
steps.content: The underlined claim attributes backlash to the art's own unconventional nature—public art that isn't sufficiently bland provokes backlash by failing to appease everyone.

steps.title: Apply Text 2's evidence to the correct answer
steps.content:
The matching choice asserts that reactions to controversial public art aren't exclusively from inherent attributes of the works.

Text 2 directly supports this: critics emphasize that poor integration and lack of viewer “priming” in non-aesthetic spaces determine reception as much as the work's provocativeness does.

The same provocative work succeeds in museums, proving context is not secondary to the work's nature.

The other choices either deny unconventionality's role (A), contradict Text 2 (B), or target a different claim (C).

D is correct.
